zh.lifeSummary: 當Hugh Bigod Chief Justice of England於1212年出生在英國英格蘭諾福爾可特福德時，他的父親Hugh le Bigod, Earl of Norfolk26歲，母親Maud Matilda Marshall20歲。他於1244年2月5日之前與Joan de Stuteville Heiress of Cottingham在英國結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和4個女兒的父母。他去世於1266年5月7日之前在他的家鄉，埋葬在英國諾福爾可特福德。

nameMeanings.name: Bigod
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Jaroslaw, Jozef, Krystyna, Slawomir.

Polish and Jewish (from Poland): from bigos, formerlydenoting anything that was chopped or slashed, now a particular dishcomposed of hash and sauerkraut; also ‘confusion’; hence perhaps anickname for someone who caused confusion.

nameMeanings.name: Hugh
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French name, Hugues, Hugo, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from hug ‘heart, mind, spirit’. It was originally a short form of various compound names containing this element. This was borne by the aristocracy of medieval France, adopted by the Normans, and introduced by them to Britain. It was consistently one of the most popular boys' names in the Middle Ages in England, but fell out of favour in the 16th century until it was revived in the 20th century. Little Hugh of Lincoln was a child supposed in the Middle Ages to have been murdered by Jews in about 1255 , a legend responsible for several outbursts of anti-Semitism at various times. The story is referred to by Chaucer in The Prioress's Tale. He is not to be confused with St Hugh of Lincoln ( 1140–1200 ), bishop of Lincoln ( 1186–1200 ), who was noted for his charity and good works, his piety, and his defence of the Church against the State. Since the later Middle Ages Hugh has also been used as an Anglicized form of the Gaelic name Aodh .

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

en.lifeSummary: When Hugh Bigod Chief Justice of England was born in 1212, in Thetford, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom, his father, Hugh le Bigod, Earl of Norfolk, was 26 and his mother, Maud Matilda Marshall, was 20. He married Joan de Stuteville Heiress of Cottingham before 5 February 1244, in England. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 4 daughters. He died before 7 May 1266, in his hometown, and was buried in Thetford, Norfolk, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Hugh Bigod Chief Justice of England est né en 1212, à Thetford, Norfolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Hugh le Bigod, Earl of Norfolk, avait 26 ans et sa mère, Maud Matilda Marshall, avait 20 ans. Il a épousé Joan de Stuteville Heiress of Cottingham avant 5 février 1244, à Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 4 filles. Il est décédé avant 7 mai 1266, dans sa ville natale, et a été enterré à Thetford, Norfolk, Angleterre.

de.Tintern Abbey, Gloucestershire, England,: Tintern Abbey, Gloucestershire, England, United Kingdom
de.lifeSummary: Als Hugh Bigod Chief Justice of England 1212 in Thetford, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater Hugh le Bigod, Earl of Norfolk 26 und seine Mutter Maud Matilda Marshall 20. Er hat vor 5. Februar 1244 in England Joan de Stuteville Heiress of Cottingham geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 4 Töchter. Er starb vor 7. Mai 1266 in seinem Geburtsort und wurde in Thetford, Norfolk, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Hugh Bigod Chief Justice of England è nato il nel 1212 nel Thetford, Norfolk, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, Hugh le Bigod, Earl of Norfolk, aveva 26 anni e il suo madre, Maud Matilda Marshall, aveva 20 anni. Lui sposato Joan de Stuteville Heiress of Cottingham prima del 5 febbraio 1244, nel England. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 4 figlie. Morì prima del 7 maggio 1266, nel sua cittadina, e fu sepolto a Thetford, Norfolk, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Hugh Bigod Chief Justice of Englandが1212年にイギリスイギリスノーフォルクスエトフォードで生まれた時，父Hugh le Bigod, Earl of Norfolkは26歳，母Maud Matilda Marshallは20歳であった。1244年2月5日以前にイギリスでJoan de Stuteville Heiress of Cottinghamと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。彼は1266年5月7日以前に故郷で死去し，イギリスノーフォルクスエトフォードにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Hugh Bigod Chief Justice of England(이)가 1212년 영국 잉글랜드 노르폴크 테트퍼드에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Hugh le Bigod, Earl of Norfolk(은)는 26세였고, 그의 어머니 Maud Matilda Marshall(은)는 20세였다. 그는 1244년 2월 5일 이전 영국에서 Joan de Stuteville Heiress of Cottingham(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1266년 5월 7일 이전 그의 고향에서 사망했으며 영국 노르폴크 테트퍼드에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Hugh Bigod Chief Justice of England nasceu em 1212, em Thetford, Norfolk, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Hugh le Bigod, Earl of Norfolk, tinha 26 anos e sua mãe, Maud Matilda Marshall, tinha 20 anos. Ele casou-se com Joan de Stuteville Heiress of Cottingham antes 5 de fevereiro de 1244, em Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 4 filhas. Ele faleceu antes 7 de maio de 1266, em sua cidade natal, e foi sepultado em Thetford, Norfolk, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Hugh Bigod Chief Justice of England родился в 1212 г. в местности тетфорд, норфолк, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Hugh le Bigod, Earl of Norfolk было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали Maud Matilda Marshall, 20. Он женился на женщине по имени Joan de Stuteville Heiress of Cottingham перед 5 февраль 1244 г. в местности Англия. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 4 дочери. Он умер перед 7 май 1266 г. в его родном городе и был похоронен в местности тетфорд, норфолк, Англия.

Se casó con Joan de Stuteville Heiress of Cottingham antes de 5 de febrero de 1244, en Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 4 hijas. Murió antes de 7 de mayo de 1266, en su pueblo, y fue sepultado en Thetford, Norfolk, Inglaterra.
